---
title: "Projects"
---

Research projects led by or affiliated with the NSW Child E Cohort Program, based on the [research activities](https://www.unsw.edu.au/staff/kathleen-falster) of Program Lead Dr Kathleen Falster.

## Multi-jurisdictional grant-funded projects

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.info-card}
### The HEAL E-Cohort Study (2025–2028)
Transforming health and wellbeing outcomes from injury for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children, led by Dr Falster as Chief Investigator for the NSW linked-data stream of a joint NSW–South Australia project.

**Funding:** NHMRC Clinical Trials and Cohort Studies Grant ($4M)
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Health Needs of Children in Out-of-Home Care (2023–2027)
Investigates whether the health needs of the roughly 50,000 children in out-of-home care across Australia are being met, with Dr Falster leading the NSW stream of a joint NSW–South Australia project.

**Funding:** MRFF Research Data Infrastructure Grant ($2.4M)
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Cross-Jurisdictional E-Cohorts for Child Maltreatment (2020–2025)
Examines how the health system responds to child maltreatment and makes the case for building cross-jurisdictional e-cohorts to better inform that response.

**Funding:** NHMRC Clinical Trials and Cohort Studies Grant ($1,248,388)
:::

:::

## NSW-specific grant-funded projects

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.info-card}
### First 2000 Days Program (2024–2027)
Informs policy and service delivery for mothers, babies, children, and families during the first 2000 days of life.

**Funding:** NSW Health ($350,000)
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Aboriginal Child Restoration from Out-of-Home Care (2021–2026)
Examines pathways to successful restoration of Aboriginal children from out-of-home care, with Dr Falster leading the data linkage stream of a mixed-methods project.

**Funding:** ARC Discovery Indigenous Grant ($427,685)
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Sustaining NSW Families Evaluation (2021–2025)
Evaluates the impact of the Sustaining NSW Families program on child development and family wellbeing outcomes, with Dr Falster leading the outcome evaluation.

**Funding:** NSW Health tender ($400,000)
:::

::: {.info-card}
### ACCESS: Aboriginal Ear Health Support System (2020–2025)
Develops, embeds, and evaluates best-practice models of care for Aboriginal children's ear health, with Dr Falster leading the data linkage stream of a mixed-methods project.

**Funding:** MRFF Grant ($887,186)
:::

:::

## Multi-jurisdictional research using NSW Child E-Cohort data

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.info-card}
### Bring Them Home, Keep Them Home
Uses qualitative and quantitative research methods to inform the prevention of child removals and early restoration of Aboriginal children from out-of-home care.

**Funding:** ARC-funded
**Collaborators:** Led by Associate Professor BJ Newton
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Child Protection System Response Study
Informs and evaluates policy and service delivery responses for children and families involved with child protection services across NSW and South Australia.

**Funding:** NHMRC-funded
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Injury and Hospitalisation Study
Informs policy and service delivery responses to support hospitalised injury and subsequent outcomes for Aboriginal children across NSW and South Australia.

**Funding:** NHMRC-funded
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Families with Multiple and Complex Needs
Studies families with multiple and complex needs across NSW, South Australia, and Western Australia.

**Funding:** ARC-funded
**Collaborators:** Australian Centre for Child Protection, University of Adelaide
:::

:::

## Prior NHMRC Early Career Fellowship research

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.info-card}
### Seeding Success Study
Population-based cohort research using linked administrative data to examine factors contributing to positive early childhood health and development outcomes among Aboriginal and non-Aboriginal children in NSW.

**Funding:** NHMRC-funded
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Indigenous Health Outcomes Patient Evaluation (IHOPE)
Examines inequities between Aboriginal and non-Aboriginal children in NSW in potentially avoidable hospitalisations, surgical procedures for middle ear disease, and hospitalisations for unintentional injuries.

**Funding:** NHMRC-funded
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Study of Environment on Aboriginal Resilience and Child Health (SEARCH)
Generates evidence on injuries, asthma, ear health, and hospitalisations among Aboriginal children attending community-controlled health services in major cities and inner regional centres of NSW.

**Funding:** NHMRC-funded
**Collaborators:** SAX Institute
:::

:::
